description_long: This is a vintage black corded telephone with a robust rectangular base and a classic rotary dial at the front center. The handset rests on a cradle at the top, with a visible wired cord connecting it to the base, looping loosely to the side. The rotary dial features metallic finger holes and a central label area, showing signs of wear and age. The body of the phone is made of a hard, glossy plastic or Bakelite material, with metallic components for the dial and internal mechanisms. The overall design is utilitarian, with minimal ornamentation, reflecting mid-20th-century telephone aesthetics. The phone is compact and sturdy, suitable for placement on a desk or table in a home or office setting.
description: A vintage black corded telephone with a rotary dial, sturdy rectangular base, and a wired handset resting on a cradle. The phone features a glossy finish and a coiled cord, evoking classic mid-century design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the rotary dial, handset on cradle, and the cord extending to the left.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the handset's profile and the smooth, curved top of the base.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the handset and the cord trailing off to the right.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the cord looping forward and the rotary dial partially visible.
